heading: Authority to enter into contracts with State and local governmental entities
label: 1856a–1 Authority to enter into contracts with State and local governmental entities
text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, in fiscal year 1992 and thereafter, the Secretary of the Interior, the Secretary of Agriculture, the Secretary of Energy, the Secretary of the Army, and the Secretary of the Smithsonian Institution are authorized to enter into contracts with State and local governmental entities, including local fire districts, for procurement of services in the presuppression, detection, and suppression of fires on any units within their jurisdiction.
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Department of the Interior and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1992, and not as part of act , which comprises this subchapter.May 27, 1955
Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in the following prior appropriation act: , , .Pub. L. 101–512, title III, § 310104 Stat. 1959Nov. 5, 1990
Amendments2007— inserted “the Secretary of the Army,” after “the Secretary of Energy,”.Pub. L. 110–114
